steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage argues that history cannot stay confined to academics because it is essential to democratic function. The final sentence ("History is too ______ to leave to historians alone") must carry that sense of urgency: history is so important that it belongs to everyone, not just experts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "accessible": "Too accessible to leave to historians" contradicts the argument; if history is already easy to reach, there is no urgency to take it away from academics. Wrong direction. ✗
  • "complex": "Too complex to leave to historians" would imply historians cannot handle it, which is incoherent. And it doesn't support the argument that the public needs history. ✗
  • "critical": "Too critical to leave to historians alone" means too crucially important to restrict to specialists. This matches the argument that public historical understanding is essential to democracy and an electorate's functioning. ✓
  • "respectable": "Too respectable to leave to historians" is nearly meaningless in context; respectability gives no reason to broaden access. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
